sections.label: Section 574
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν ἄλλη σή τις ἀντʼ ἐμοῦ γυνή .
sections.translation: You have no other wife but me .

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: οὔ που φρονῶ μὲν εὖ , τὸ δʼ ὄμμα μου νοσεῖ ;
sections.translation: Can it be that I am in my right mind , but my sight is failing ?

sections.label: Section 576
sections.original: οὐ γάρ με λεύσσων σὴν δάμαρθʼ ὁρᾶν δοκεῖς ;
sections.translation: Don ’ t you think that when you look at me you see your wife ?

sections.label: Section 577
sections.original: τὸ σῶμʼ ὅμοιον , τὸ δὲ σαφές μʼ ἀποστερεῖ .
sections.translation: Your body resembles hers , but the real truth robs me of this belief .

sections.label: Section 578
sections.original: σκέψαι · τί σοὐνδεῖ ; τίς δὲ σοῦ σοφώτερος ;
sections.translation: Look ; what more do you need ? Who knows better than you ?

sections.label: Section 579
sections.original: ἔοικας · οὔτοι τοῦτό γʼ ἐξαρνήσομαι .
sections.translation: You are like her ; I will not deny that at least .

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: τίς οὖν διδάξει σʼ ἄλλος ἢ τὰ σʼ ὄμματα ;
sections.translation: Who then shall teach you , if not your own eyes ?

sections.label: Section 581
sections.original: ἐκεῖ νοσοῦμεν , ὅτι δάμαρτʼ ἄλλην ἔχω .
sections.translation: It is there that I am ailing , because I have another wife .

sections.label: Section 582
sections.original: οὐκ ἦλθον ἐς γῆν Τρῳάδʼ , ἀλλʼ εἴδωλον ἦν .
sections.translation: I did not go to Troy ; that was a phantom .

sections.label: Section 583
sections.original: καὶ τίς βλέποντα σώματʼ ἐξεργάζεται ;
sections.translation: And who fashions living bodies ?

sections.label: Section 584
sections.original: αἰθήρ , ὅθεν σὺ θεοπόνητʼ ἔχεις λέχη .
sections.translation: The air , out of which you have a wife that the gods labored over .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: τίνος πλάσαντος θεῶν ; ἄελπτα γὰρ λέγεις .
sections.translation: What god ’ s handiwork ? You are saying things beyond hope .

sections.label: Section 586
sections.original: Ἥρας , διάλλαγμʼ , ὡς Πάρις με μὴ λάβοι .
sections.translation: Hera ’ s , as a substitute , so that Paris would not have me .

sections.label: Section 587
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἂν ἐνθάδʼ ἦσθά τʼ ἐν Τροίᾳ θʼ ἅμα ;
sections.translation: How then could you be here and in Troy at the same time ?

sections.label: Section 588
sections.original: τοὔνομα γένοιτʼ ἂν πολλαχοῦ , τὸ σῶμα δʼ οὔ .
sections.translation: The name may be in many places , though not the body .

sections.label: Section 589
sections.original: μέθες με , λύπης ἅλις ἔχων ἐλήλυθα .
sections.translation: Let me go ! I have come here with enough pain .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: λείψεις γὰρ ἡμᾶς , τὰ δὲ κένʼ ἐξάξεις λέχη ;
sections.translation: Will you leave me , and take that phantom bride away ?

sections.label: Section 591
sections.original: καὶ χαῖρέ γʼ , Ἑλένῃ προσφερὴς ὁθούνεκʼ εἶ .
sections.translation: Yes , and fare well , for your likeness to Helen .

sections.label: Section 592
sections.original: ἀπωλόμην · λαβοῦσά σʼ οὐχ ἕξω πόσιν .
sections.translation: I am ruined ! I found you , my husband , but I will not have you .

sections.label: Section 593
sections.original: τοὐκεῖ με μέγεθος τῶν πόνων πείθει , σὺ δʼ οὔ .
sections.translation: The greatness of my troubles over there convinces me ; you do not .

sections.label: Section 594
sections.original: οἲ ἐγώ · τίς ἡμῶν ἐγένετʼ ἀθλιωτέρα ;
sections.translation: Ah me ! Who was ever more miserable than I am ?

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: οἱ φίλτατοι λείπουσί μʼ οὐδʼ ἀφίξομαι
sections.translation: Those whom I love best are leaving me , and I shall never reach the Hellenes or my own country .

sections.label: Section 597
sections.original: Μενέλαε , μαστεύων σε κιγχάνω μόλις
sections.translation: ( entering hurriedly .) Menelaos , I find you , after taking great trouble to look for you , wandering over the whole of this foreign land ; I am sent by the comrades whom you left behind .

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ; οὔ που βαρβάρων συλᾶσθʼ ὕπο ;
sections.translation: What is it ? Surely you are not being plundered by the foreigners ?

sections.label: Section 601
sections.original: θαῦμʼ ἔστʼ , ἔλασσον τοὔνομʼ ἢ τὸ πρᾶγμʼ ἔχον .
sections.translation: It is a miracle ; what I say is of less account than what happened .

sections.label: Section 602
sections.original: λέγʼ · ὡς φέρεις τι τῇδε τῇ σπουδῇ νέον .
sections.translation: Tell me ; for , judging by this eagerness , you are certainly bringing something new .

sections.label: Section 603
sections.original: λέγω πόνους σε μυρίους τλῆναι μάτην .
sections.translation: I say that you have suffered countless labors in vain .

sections.label: Section 604
sections.original: παλαιὰ θρηνεῖς πήματʼ · ἀγγέλλεις δὲ τί ;
sections.translation: You are mourning over old sorrows ; what is your message ?

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: βέβηκεν ἄλοχος σὴ πρὸς αἰθέρος πτυχὰς
sections.translation: Your wife has disappeared , taken up into the folds of the unseen air ; she is hidden in heaven , and as she left the hallowed cave where we were keeping her , she said this : Miserable Phrygians , and all the Achaeans ! On my account you were dying by the banks of Skamandros ,

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: ἀκταῖσιν Ἥρας μηχαναῖς ἐθνῄσκετε ,
sections.translation: through Hera ’ s contrivance , for you thought that Paris had Helen when he didn ’ t . But I , since I have stayed my appointed time , and kept the laws of fate , will now depart into the sky , my father ; but the unhappy daughter of Tyndareus ,

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: ἄλλως κακὰς ἤκουσεν οὐδὲν αἰτία .
sections.translation: guilty in no way , has borne an evil name without reason .

sections.label: Section 616
sections.original: ὦ χαῖρε , Λήδας θύγατερ , ἐνθάδʼ ἦσθʼ ἄρα ;
sections.translation: Welcome , daughter of Leda , were you here after all ? I was just announcing your departure up to the hidden starry realms , not knowing that you had a winged body . I will not let you mock us like this again ,

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: ἡμᾶς τόδʼ αὖθις , ὡς ἄδην ἐν Ἰλίῳ
sections.translation: for you gave your fill of trouble to your husband and his allies in Ilion .

sections.label: Section 622
sections.original: τοῦτʼ ἔστʼ ἐκεῖνο · ξυμβεβᾶσιν οἱ λόγοι
sections.translation: This is the meaning of that ; her words have turned out to be true . O longed-for day , that has given you to my arms !

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ ἀνδρῶν Μενέλεως , ὁ μὲν χρόνος
sections.translation: O Menelaos , dearest of men , the time was long , but delight is just now ours .

sections.label: Section 627
sections.original: ἔλαβον ἀσμένα πόσιν ἐμόν , φίλαι ,
sections.translation: With joy I have found my husband , friends , I have embraced my dear one , after long days of blazing light .

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: κἀγὼ σέ · πολλοὺς δʼ ἐν μέσῳ λόγους ἔχων
sections.translation: And I have found you ; but I have many questions about t ; now I do not know what to begin with first .

sections.label: Section 632
sections.original: γέγηθα , κρατὶ δʼ ὀρθίους ἐθείρας
sections.translation: I am so happy , the hair rises on my head and my tears run down . I fling my arms around your neck ,

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: ὦ πόσις , ὡς λάβω .
sections.translation: dear husband , to have my delight .

sections.label: Section 636
sections.original: ὦ φιλτάτη πρόσοψις , οὐκ ἐμέμφθην ·
sections.translation: O dearest sight ! I have no fault to find : I have my wife , the daughter of Zeus and Leda ; your brothers on their snow-white steeds

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: ξυνομαίμονες ὤλβισαν ὤλβισαν
sections.translation: blessed you , blessed you at an earlier time , while torches blazed , but the god who took you from my home is driving us on to another fortune , better than this . An evil that was good brought you together with me , your husband

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: χρόνιον , ἀλλʼ ὅμως ὀναίμαν τύχας .
sections.translation: after a long time , but may I still benefit by my good luck .

sections.label: Section 646
sections.original: ὄναιο δῆτα . ταὐτὰ δὴ ξυνεύχομαι ·
sections.translation: May you benefit indeed , and I join in the same prayer ; for when there are two , it is not possible for one to be unhappy and the other not .

sections.label: Section 648
sections.original: φίλαι φίλαι , τὰ πάρος οὐκέτι
sections.translation: My dear friends , I no longer sigh or grieve over what is past .

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: πόσιν ἐμὸν ἔχομεν ἔχομεν , ὃν ἔμενον
sections.translation: I have my husband , for whom I have been waiting to come from Troy for many years .

sections.label: Section 652
sections.original: ἔχεις , ἐγώ τε σέ · ἡλίους δὲ μυρίους
sections.translation: You have me , and I have you ; although it was hard to live through so many days , I now understand the actions of the goddess . My joy is tearful ; it has more

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: χάριτος ἢ λύπας .
sections.translation: delight than sorrow .

sections.label: Section 656
sections.original: τί φῶ ; τίς ἂν τάδʼ ἤλπισεν βροτῶν ποτε ;
sections.translation: What can I say ? What mortal could ever have hoped for this ? I hold you to my heart , little as I ever thought to .

sections.label: Section 658
sections.original: κἀγὼ σὲ τὴν δοκοῦσαν Ἰδαίαν πόλιν
sections.translation: And I hold you , whom we thought to have gone to Ida ’ s city and the unhappy towers of Ilion .

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν , δόμων πῶς τῶν ἐμῶν ἀπεστάλης ;
sections.translation: By the gods , how were you taken from my home ?

sections.label: Section 661
sections.original: ἒ ἔ · πικρὰς ἐς ἀρχὰς βαίνεις ,
sections.translation: Ah ! ah ! You are setting out on a bitter beginning . Ah ! ah ! You are asking about a bitter tale .

sections.label: Section 663
sections.original: λέγʼ · ὡς ἀκουστὰ πάντα δῶρα δαιμόνων .
sections.translation: Speak ; all gifts from the gods should be heard .

sections.label: Section 664
sections.original: ἀπέπτυσα μὲν λόγον , οἷον οἷον ἐσοίσομαι .
sections.translation: I detest the story I am now to introduce .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: ὅμως δὲ λέξον · ἡδύ τοι μόχθων κλύειν .
sections.translation: Tell it anyway . It is sweet to hear of troubles .

sections.label: Section 666
sections.original: οὐκ ἐπὶ βαρβάρου λέκτρα νεανία
sections.translation: Not to the bed of the young barbarian , on the wings of oars , on the wings of desire for lawless marriage —

sections.label: Section 669
sections.original: τίς γάρ σε δαίμων ἢ πότμος συλᾷ πάτρας ;
sections.translation: What god or fate tore you from your country ?

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: ὁ Διὸς ὁ Διός , ὦ πόσι , παῖς μʼ ⋯
sections.translation: Ah , my husband ! The son of Zeus , of Zeus , brought me to the Nile .

sections.label: Section 672
sections.original: θαυμαστά · τοῦ πέμψαντος ; ὦ δεινοὶ λόγοι .
sections.translation: Amazing ! Who sent you there ? O dreadful story !

sections.label: Section 673
sections.original: κατεδάκρυσα καὶ βλέφαρον ὑγραίνω
sections.translation: I have wept bitterly , and my eyes are wet with tears ; the wife of Zeus ruined me .

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: Ἥρα ; τί νῷν χρῄζουσα προσθεῖναι κακόν ;
sections.translation: Hera ? Why did she want to bring trouble to the two of us ?
